perturbation.original_text: Regarding t he possible entry into a business relationship, each party may disclose its Trade Secrets or Confidential Information to the other. For and in consideration of the disclosures to be made hereunder and the mutual promises and covenants expressed herein, the parties agree as follows: I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean any information and data of a confidential or proprietary nature which is disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, including but not li mited to, customer information, proprietary technical, financial, personnel, marketing, pricing, sales, and/or commercial information with respect to computer networking, data communications, computing services; development, operation, performance, cost, k now -how, business, process and marketing of computer software and other technology relating to computer networking, data communications and computing services as well as ideas, concepts, designs and inventions, computer source and object code and computer programming techniques; and all record bearing media containing or disclosing such information and techniques that are disclosed pursuant to this Agreement. The terms and existence of this Agreement, the fact that Confidential Information has been made av ailable hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a potential business relationship involving the Parties and all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall also be conside red Confidential Information that is subject to the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Regarding t he possible entry into a business relationship, each party may disclose its Trade Secrets or Confidential Information to the other. For and in consideration of the disclosures to be made hereunder and the mutual promises and covenants expressed herein, the parties agree as follows: I. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean any information and data of a confidential or proprietary nature which is disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, including but not li mited to, customer information, proprietary technical, financial, personnel, marketing, pricing, sales, and/or commercial information with respect to computer networking, data communications, computing services; development, operation, performance, cost, k now -how, business, process and marketing of computer software and other technology relating to computer networking, data communications and computing services as well as ideas, concepts, designs and inventions, computer source and object code and computer programming techniques; and all record bearing media containing or disclosing such information and techniques that are disclosed pursuant to this Agreement. The terms and existence of this Agreement, the fact that Confidential Information has been made av ailable hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a potential business relationship involving the Parties and all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall not be considered Confidential Information that is subject to the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that discussions or negotiations taking place are not considered Confidential Information, it creates an in-text contradiction with the earlier part of the same section I where it states 'all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall also be considered Confidential Information'. This introduces uncertainty about whether ongoing discussions are protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - As used herein, Confidential Information shall mean any information and data of a confidential or proprietary nature which is disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, including but not li mited to, customer information, proprietary technical, financial, personnel, marketing, pricing, sales, and/or commercial information with respect to computer networking, data communications, computing services; development, operation, performance, cost, k now -how, business, process and marketing of computer software and other technology relating to computer networking, data communications and computing services as well as ideas, concepts, designs and inventions, computer source and object code and computer programming techniques; and all record bearing media containing or disclosing such information and techniques that are disclosed pursuant to this Agreement. The terms and existence of this Agreement, the fact that Confidential Information has been made av ailable hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a potential business relationship involving the Parties and all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall not be considered Confidential Information that is subject to the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that certain discussions or negotiations are *not* considered confidential information. The contradicted text (which is the same text after modification) includes the clause that 'all the terms, conditions, and other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof) shall also be considered Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction within the section I itself, as what is and isn't confidential information is being defined in two conflicting ways. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of each Party. No copying: Both further agree that they will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of stated parties. No contact: both further agree that they will not directly contact any listed source of Confidential Information without the prior written consent of respective parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. No copying: Both further agree that they will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of stated parties. No contact: both further agree that they will not directly contact any listed source of Confidential Information without the prior written consent of respective parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'each Party' to 'Disclosing Party' for disclosing confidential information creates an in-text contradiction. Now, only the Disclosing Party's consent is needed, conflicting with the implied requirement of consent from both parties for protecting information, as stated in the overall agreement's purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROTECTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION - Both parties understand and acknowledge that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by stated parties by in vestment of significant time, effort and exposure, and that the confidential information is a valuable, special and unique asset of stated parties which provides each with a significant competitive advantage. Therefore, stated parties agree to hold in conf idence and not to disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. No copying: Both further agree that they will not copy or modify any Confidential Information without the prior written consent of stated parties. No contact: both further agree that they will not directly contact any listed source of Confidential Information without the prior written consent of respective parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'each Party' requires consent, whereas the changed text states 'Disclosing Party' requires consent. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, which creates a contradiction because the Contradicted Text includes the 'Disclosing Party', implying that the other party does not need to give concent for the release of information. This contradicts the original intent of both parties protecting information, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, which specifies that the original agreement's purpose includes consent from both parties, fitting the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any hau lers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going or future transaction or project.
perturbation.changed_text: III. NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going transaction or project.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'future' before 'transaction or project' in the non-circumvent clause creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement now protects only ongoing transactions, conflicting with the intent to prevent circumvention in future dealings established in the overall agreement's scope. This introduces ambiguity about the extent of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON -CIRCUMVENT - The Parties, hereby irrevocably agree, an d guarantee each other they shall not, directly or indirectly interfere with, circumvent or attempt to circumvent, avoid, by -pass, or obviate each other's interest, or the interest or relationship between the Parties with other haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, , government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, developers or manufactur ers, to change, increase or avoid directly or indirectly payment of established or to be established fees, commissions, or continuance of pre - established relationship or intervene in non -contracted relationships that by -pass one of the Parties with any haulers, trucking companies, contractors on project, future contractors on project, carriers, prime contractors, sub - contractors, producers, sellers, buyers, brokers, dealers, government departments, distributors, financial institutions, technology owners, d evelopers or manufacturers, corporations, producer, technology owner, partnership, or individual revealed or introduced by one of the Parties to one another in connection with any on -going transaction or project.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Non-Circumvent clause applies to both 'on-going or future transaction or project'. The changed text removes the word 'future', so the clause now only applies to 'on-going transaction or project'. The contradicted text is similar to the changed text, as it also only mentions 'on-going transaction or project', therefore, the change doesn't cause a contradiction. However, the explanation states that by removing 'future' before 'transaction or project' in the non-circumvent clause creates an in-text contradiction, which means the original intent of the agreement to protect against circumvention in future dealings is compromised. This discrepancy creates ambiguity regarding the extent of protection offered by the agreement. Therefore the change creates a strong in-text contradiction. Since the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction says 'Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**.', in this case, it leads to a contradiction because the clause, as modified, conflicts with the overall intent and scope of the original agreement which intended to include future transactions as well.

perturbation.original_text: The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose, other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'other than the purpose authorized in writing by the Providing Party to the benefit of the joint business effort of the parties' it creates a contradiction regarding the allowed use of proprietary information. The original text allows use with written authorization for the joint business effort, while the modified text forbids any use except for the Receiving Party's own purposes, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AGREEMENT - This mutual non -circumvent and non -disclosure agreement is for the purpose of protecting new business developed jointly by the Parties therein. The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose. Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this Agreement, both p arties herein will hold in confidence and not disclose, reproduce, distribute, transmit, reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or transfer, directly or indirectly, in any form, by any means, or for any purpose, the Trade Secrets or Confidential Informa tion or any portion thereof. The Receiving Party may only disclose the Trade Secrets and Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants and employees, to the extent such persons have a need to know such information for the purposes described in th is Agreement, and provided each such employee shall be obligated in writing to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement and each such attorney or accountant shall either be legally bound to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreeme nt or so obligated in writing. Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement with regard to the Trade Secrets remain in effect for so long as such information shall remain a trade secret under applicable law. Recipient’s obligations with regard to the Conf idential Information shall remain in effect for two (2) years after the execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the use of proprietary information with written authorization for the joint business effort. The changed text removes this exception, forbidding any use except for the Receiving Party's own purposes. The contradicted text which includes the sentence: 'The Undersigned agree that they shall not use proprietary information or trade secrets unknown to the general public belonging to the Providing Party for their own use or for any purpose.' creates an in-text contradiction by implication with the 'purpose' and agreement's goal to be joint business development. Now it's forbidden to use it for the agreement's joint business development purposes, thus leading to a strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Upon demand by either of the Parties, the Receiving Party herein Undersigned will return all the proprietary information to the appropriate Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon demand by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party herein Undersigned will return all the proprietary information to the appropriate Provider.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'either of the Parties' to 'the Disclosing Party' in the proprietary information return clause creates an in-text contradiction. The modified text implies only the Disclosing Party can demand the return, conflicting with the overall agreement's intent that both parties have equal rights to protect their information. The Recieving Party might also need the information back but can't ask for it back.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Undersigned agrees that no copies in any form whether magnetic, printed or electronic, abstracts or photogr aphs shall be made without written permission of the Provider {defined in III b}. Upon demand by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party herein Undersigned will return all the proprietary information to the appropriate Provider.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'either of the Parties' can demand the return of proprietary information. The changed text modifies this to 'the Disclosing Party'. The contradicted text includes the changed text. The change creates an in-text contradiction because it restricts the ability to demand the return of proprietary information to only the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the implied equal rights of both parties in the agreement to protect their information, as the Recieving Party may need the information back but is now unable to request it, creating a clear inconsistency as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Disclosing Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Receiving Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Receiving Party' for bringing an enforcement action while keeping 'Disclosing Party' as the one entitled to relief creates an in-text contradiction. It's now unclear if the Receiving Party also benefits from the clause, undermining their ability to protect their interests, due to the clause stating damages only to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDIES - The p arties herein acknowledge that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the injured party shall be entitled to injunctive relief as a cumulative and not necessarily successive remedy and without the need to post bond. Both parties understand, acknowled ge and agree that in the event the Receiving Party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement through a Temporary Restraining Order or other remedy, the damages to Disclosing Parry for improper disclosure of the Information are irreparable, and the Disclosing Party is entitled to equitable relief, including a preliminary injunction, in addition to other monetary relief. If any term, provision, covenant, or condition of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or a regulatory agency having jurisdiction over the subject matter herein to be enforceable, void or invalid, the remainder of the provisions herein shall remain in full force and effect and shall in no way be affected, impaired or invalidates an d the rights and obligations of the parties of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Disclosing Party' with 'Receiving Party' in the context of bringing an enforcement action, while the right to equitable relief remains with the 'Disclosing Party'. This directly contradicts the 'REMEDIES' section by creating ambiguity and inconsistency regarding which party benefits from the clause when the Receiving Party brings the enforcement action. It leads to a strong in-text contradiction because it's now unclear if the Receiving Party is entitled to the remedies outlined, undermining their ability to protect their interests, aligning with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
